en_body:
(Respectfully and dearly sent to Poet Emeritus Hoàng Mai)


 The longer I live, the deeper I comprehend the grace of Humanity, and the precious lessons Life has taught me, through ten thousand trials of delirious happiness and deeply saturated suffering, those ultimate emotions and thoughts excavating the soul until the Heart's voice bursts forth..!!

 Perhaps, most precious is compassion with our fellow-humans, that we may learn Humility! Happiness and abundance easily create in human beings self-pride and faith in overzealous or extreme molds of moral value. To live long is to recognize and feel the finitude of humankind, especially within the intention to transcend that finitude..!!

 From Lord Jesus, the Ancestral Buddha and the Bodhisattvas, Lao Tzu, Confucius, saints, sages, philosophers, scholars, exemplary masters who kept the Precepts, incorruptible and righteous statesmen, heroic and honorable military leaders, to artists who love human beings and love Life through ten thousand forms of creative Art: The positive longing to contribute one's whole mind and body to humankind, every living creature, preserving Life as Nature or villages, is infinitely precious! With a Vow of Consecration as Salvation, the great beings exhausted all their strength, forgetting everything, to seek the ultimate and most final Principle by which they might help their fellow-humans create happiness together, and find Absolute Salvation! How many people, since the human individual first learned to aspire toward something holier than all ordinary happiness before the eyes: an original and Absolute Truth: like the Buddhas or Lord Jesus Christ, abandoned even the throne; worldly power; material happiness; the ordinary safety of an ordinary living being; the peace of the mind; the temptations of worldly seductions: all were as passages, obstacles for tempering oneself to transcend...As necessary lessons requiring cultivation and study; experiences of Light and Darkness that must be lived/died utterly and wholly to distill Knowledge, using one's own body-mind to consecrate every filament within the tender Silkworm of soul and thought to all humankind and every living being..!!

 Only after passing through deeply saturated impasses, after lying faint beneath hopeless endurance because one can no longer resist by dreams, hope, or delusion, blindness: when the Truth of one's own finitude presses close against every thing/every false faith in illusions of the fragile power of one living being among the layered powers of infinite Life: suddenly we revere, love, and cherish beyond measure those supernal beings worthy of worship for that One-Vow-Of-Salvation so vast..!!

 And Life's Grace, like a stern Father-Mother: "Bitter medicine cures the sickness!" That at last we understand the center and the ending are Love and Faith! Thank you, human one! Thank you, Life! Thank you, Principle of Being! Thank you, friends of prose/poetry! And...thank you also to myself always: For loving too much, like a wild animal lying alone to lick its wounds—and what creature has never once bled, stung beneath torn skin..!!
note: The silkworm image evokes the self-consuming labor of spinning one's entire being into a gift, braided here with Buddhist vows and Christ's renunciation.
